summary.intent: Fix a GitHub Actions CI workflow for a Claude Code plugin validation PR (#90) so that the `claude` binary is accessible across workflow steps after installation.
summary.outcome: Consolidated multi-step PATH setup into a single workflow step using `export PATH` to work around `$GITHUB_PATH` not propagating between steps; committed and pushed the fix.

repo: PR #90 is in the ronnnnn/cc repo on branch deps-renovate-bun-1.x
The project uses bun for package management; global installs are done via `bun install -g` and the bin path retrieved with `bun pm bin -g`
CLAUDE.md documents the installation method and must stay in sync with actual CI workflow steps
devin-ai-integration bot auto-reviews PRs and flags drift between CLAUDE.md and workflow files

code.finding: Using $GITHUB_PATH to expose an installed binary across separate steps fails silently; consolidating install + use into a single step with `export PATH` is the reliable fix

workflow: $GITHUB_PATH writes are not reflected within the same job step that writes them, and may not propagate to subsequent steps in some runner configurations
Bot reviewer comments (devin-ai-integration) can be replied to and resolved programmatically; re-request review should be skipped for bot authors
Tracking counters (REPLIED_COMMENTS, RESOLVED_THREADS, CI_COMMITS) were maintained across the session to keep state about PR progress

friction: $GITHUB_PATH not propagating between steps caused repeated CI failures requiring multiple investigative iterations
Had to inspect multiple log targets (Install step detail, full run logs) before identifying the PATH propagation root cause
Bot-generated review comment initially looked like a real blocker but was already resolved by a prior commit — stale bot feedback created noise
open_items: Confirm CI passes after the single-step consolidation commit
Verify CLAUDE.md accurately reflects the final `bun install -g` + `bun pm bin -g` approach so no further bot drift warnings are triggered
